text.title: Survey Design Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real deployments of Survey Design. Each ties a symptom to a root cause and a concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Survey balloons to 18 questions; completion rate 28%.
antipattern.root-cause: Author tried to capture everything in one survey.
antipattern.fix: Cap at 12; split into two surveys if more needed; pilot drop-off rate.

antipattern.symptom: 'Don't you agree our AI is amazing?' yields skewed positive.
antipattern.fix: Bias-linter pattern: 'don't you', 'wouldn't you', etc; reject + rewrite neutrally.

antipattern.symptom: Per-segment claim from N=22; +/- 21% CI ignored.
antipattern.root-cause: Author cited the absolute number, not the CI.
antipattern.fix: Reject conclusion publishing below N>=120 per segment; aggregate or wait.

antipattern.symptom: Survey launched directly; first 50 responses incoherent due to ambiguous question.
antipattern.fix: Mandatory pilot N=10; rewrite anything 3+ pilots flag as confusing.
